## [00:00 - 02:46]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Let's go. So in order to evaluate your performance as a Facebook advertiser, it's really important that you have a collection of metrics that matter. Now what matters to me may not matter to you, but I often have people ask me, John, what are the metrics that you care about? So I have a saved report in my ads manager that has these 30 metrics that I can apply to any campaign no matter what the objective. And some will matter more and some less depending on what I'm optimizing for. But I want to go through what it is exactly I'm looking at. I call it, it's saved as the good stuff. So first of all includes the campaign basics, you know, typical delivery, budget, amount spent, results, cost per result. And then the reach impressions type of metrics. So reach, impressions, cost per 1000 people reached, cost per 1000 impressions or CPM and frequency. Then the various clicks. So link clicks, outbound clicks, landing page views, and then the cost per each of those three things. And then the CTR for CTR all CTR link click through rate and outbound CTR. Then some standard events, honestly, just registrations completed purchases, then the cost per both of those, then purchase conversion value, and a purchase return on ad spend. And then finally, I have some custom events are specifically for tracking a quality website visit. So a time on page event, scroll depth 70%. And then one that's 60 seconds time on page and 70% scroll depth, and then the cost per each of those three things. And that's it. That's my good stuff. But certainly find what works for you. Save it in your ads manager so it's easy to access and have fun with it. This has been a pubcast shot. Guess what? Your questions can be answered as a part of a pubcast shot. Just submit it to me at info at johnlimmer.com. Make sure you put in a subject line pubcast shot question. And maybe just maybe I'll answer your question on a pubcast shot. Thanks for joining me again today. Until next time. Do awesome things. I'm out. Okay.
